title: SonicWall Anti-Spyware IPS Alerts Correlated with Reputation (SonicWall)
dataSources: `sourcetype=dell:sonicwall:firewall` IPS/anti-spyware events; optional `threat_intel` lookup on dst_ip
spl:
index=netfw sourcetype="dell:sonicwall:firewall" earliest=-24h
| where match(_raw,"(?i)intrusion|IPS|spyware")
| lookup local_threat_intel ip AS dst_ip OUTPUT reputation score
| stats count by dst_ip, signature, reputation
| where reputation=="malicious" OR score<40 OR isnull(reputation)
| sort -count
description: IPS alerts from low-reputation destinations are higher fidelity than generic noise. This pattern prioritizes investigation queues.
value: Cuts analyst fatigue while maintaining coverage on likely malicious flows.
implementation: Maintain a simple local intel lookup or use Splunk Enterprise Security threat intelligence framework. Join dst_ip to reputation score.
visualization: Bubble chart (alert volume vs reputation), notable list for score <30.

references.title: Splunkbase — Dell SonicWall Add-on for Splunk
Splunk Connect for Syslog — Dell SonicWall reference

grandmaExplanation: IPS alerts from low-reputation destinations are higher fidelity than generic noise — we monitor SonicWall logs so teams can act before users feel pain.
